context: Use-case SOP packet for targeted MSP retrieval. Full source remains in .agents/skills/msp-provider-sop/references/msp-provider-rulebook.md and the fractal knowledgebase graph.

agentChecklist: Classify event posture before action
Create internal major incident record
Create per-tenant child records for affected customers and assign a named owner to each one
Assign incident lead, evidence owner, technical owner, comms owner, and vendor liaison when possible
Preserve evidence before disruptive cleanup unless emergency threshold is met
State next update time and decision owner
avoid: Do not mix tenant evidence or customer messages
Do not trust a suspect control plane as sole truth
Do not let one overnight engineer silently own broad impact
Do not destroy volatile evidence for cosmetic cleanup
communications: Internal bridge may include full scope; customer messages stay tenant-specific
Say what is known, unknown, actions underway, approvals needed, and next update time
decisionGates: Evidence captured or explicit emergency exception recorded
Blast radius and customer impact assessed
Senior authority activated for multi-tenant, destructive, regulated, or control-plane events
Rollback and monitoring path named before disruptive action
firstActions: Declare incident posture
Move command and scribe log outside suspect systems if needed
Open internal major incident record plus named owner per affected tenant child record
Freeze unsafe automation narrowly while preserving evidence
Wake senior incident lead when scope is multi-tenant, destructive, regulated, or control-plane related
sop.summary: Common first-hour frame for any high-risk MSP event. It supplies command structure, evidence discipline, tenant separation, and escalation gates without loading the full rulebook.
sop.useCase: MSP common major incident frame

text: Common MSP major incident SOP packet. Use for multi-tenant, destructive, regulated, after-hours, or control-plane events. It is a compact prompt packet; inspect the source rulebook through the fractal knowledgebase when full context is needed.
title: MSP common major incident frame

context: Use-case SOP packet for targeted MSP retrieval.

agentChecklist: Identify active 24/7 clients and service windows
Separate safety/life-critical impact from ordinary downtime
Preserve evidence before broad action
Name the minimum viable containment
Wake the right authority instead of expanding the bridge blindly
avoid: Do not mass isolate continuity-protected clients without owner gate
Do not use one generic customer update for all tenants
Do not wait for business hours when destructive or regulated risk is active
communications: Tell each affected customer impact, current risk, action being taken, approval needed, and next update time
Avoid confirming breach/containment until evidence supports it
decisionGates: Customer-owner approval before disruptive action on continuity-protected services
Emergency exception only when active harm is worse than waiting
Senior incident lead for cross-tenant or destructive conditions
firstActions: Build active-client impact matrix
Preserve current telemetry and control-plane state
Hold broad shutdowns until continuity gate is met
Apply narrow reversible containment to non-critical blast paths
Escalate to senior incident lead and customer-success/comms owner
sop.summary: After-hours 24/7 operations SOP for preserving safety and service continuity while still containing security or reliability risk.
sop.useCase: 24/7 operations and continuity SOP

text: 24/7 operations SOP packet for after-hours MSP incidents affecting active customer operations. Compact retrieval only; full operational policy belongs in the rulebook/fractal view.
title: 24/7 operations and continuity SOP

context: Use-case SOP packet for targeted MSP retrieval.

agentChecklist: Classify alert confidence and tenant scope
Preserve raw alert, query, timeline, and enrichment evidence
Correlate across identity, endpoint, network, cloud, and ticket context
Define containment threshold before action
Escalate if alert indicates multi-tenant control-plane or active compromise
avoid: Do not close as false positive from one green dashboard
Do not run destructive cleanup before evidence export
Do not merge tenant timelines unless internal-only and access-controlled
communications: Internal SOC notes may contain cross-tenant scope; customer notices must be tenant-specific
Use confidence labels: suspected, confirmed, contained, not contained
decisionGates: Enough evidence to classify benign/suspicious/incident
Containment is reversible or emergency threshold is documented
Customer escalation if business action or downtime is likely
firstActions: Snapshot raw alert and enrichment
Map affected tenant/assets/accounts
Check for matching events across MSP and customer telemetry
Preserve logs before response action
Open or update tenant-specific security ticket
sop.summary: SOC triage SOP for turning alerts into evidence-backed incident decisions without flattening tenant boundaries.
sop.useCase: SOC alert triage SOP

text: SOC alert triage SOP packet for MSP security operations. Compact retrieval only; full hunting/playbook docs belong in the fractal knowledgebase/source docs.
title: SOC alert triage SOP

context: Use-case SOP packet for targeted MSP retrieval.

agentChecklist: Verify requester identity and customer authority
Check ticket/change record and affected tenant
Prefer named accounts and least privilege
Record approval, action, expiry, rollback, and monitoring
Escalate suspicious or pressure-heavy requests
avoid: Do not reset MFA or grant admin access from caller pressure alone
Do not use shared cross-customer admin credentials
Do not bypass offboarding or stale-access checks
communications: Confirm action and expiry to authorized customer contact
Keep customer-specific notes in that customer's ticket only
decisionGates: Requester verified through approved channel
Approver is authorized for the tenant and action
Risky privilege has expiry and owner
Rollback or revocation path documented
firstActions: Validate caller/requester through approved channel
Locate customer tenant and ticket context
Check whether request is normal, urgent, or suspicious
Apply least-privilege change only after approval gate
Record exact action and expiry
sop.summary: Service desk SOP for access, MFA, offboarding, and customer authority checks where social pressure can create security incidents.
sop.useCase: Service desk access and authority SOP

text: Service desk access SOP packet. Compact retrieval only; full service desk KB and customer-specific procedures belong in the knowledgebase/fractal view.
title: Service desk access and authority SOP

context: Use-case SOP packet for targeted MSP retrieval.

agentChecklist: Identify subscription, tenant, resource group, app/service, and customer owner
Preserve Azure activity, Entra audit/sign-in, resource diagnostics, and deployment history
Check RBAC and managed identity changes before restarting or redeploying
Use staged rollback or traffic shift when possible
Record customer impact and recovery evidence
avoid: Do not redeploy over forensic evidence without export
Do not change NSG/firewall/DNS broadly without dependency check
Do not assume Azure portal status proves customer app health
communications: Customer-facing updates should name affected service, impact, mitigation, ETA/next update, and approval needed
Do not expose other customer/subscription details
decisionGates: Owner/authority verified for production change
Rollback artifact or prior deployment known
Database/storage impact assessed
Monitoring confirms recovery before closing
firstActions: Map affected Azure tenant/subscription/resource group
Export activity and identity logs
Check recent deployments, RBAC, secrets, network, and platform health
Stabilize with least disruptive rollback/traffic control
Open customer-specific incident/change record
sop.summary: Azure hosting SOP for MSP-managed cloud incidents and production changes, with RBAC, evidence, rollback, and customer communication gates.
sop.useCase: Azure hosting operations SOP

text: Azure hosting operations SOP packet for MSP-managed cloud services. Compact retrieval only; full architecture/runbook docs belong in the fractal knowledgebase.
title: Azure hosting operations SOP

context: Use-case SOP packet for targeted MSP retrieval.

agentChecklist: Identify client, environment, release, feature flag, data migration, and owner
Preserve deployment logs, commit/PR, CI output, config, database migration state, and customer impact
Prefer feature flag off, rollback, or hotfix depending on data risk
Separate incident, change, and customer comms records
Run post-change verification before declaring recovery
avoid: Do not hotfix production without preserving failure evidence
Do not roll back across irreversible migrations without data gate
Do not promise root cause before logs and diff are reviewed
communications: Client update should state impact, workaround, mitigation, validation, and next update
Internal notes can include suspected code/config causes; client comms should avoid unsupported blame
decisionGates: Rollback is data-safe or migration compensation is ready
Owner approves client-visible change
Monitoring and user workflow test confirm mitigation
Postmortem/action item created for recurrence risk
firstActions: Freeze further deploys for affected service
Capture release/change evidence
Assess user impact and data integrity risk
Choose feature flag rollback, version rollback, or hotfix through explicit gate
Validate customer workflow after mitigation
sop.summary: Client-facing software delivery SOP for releases, hotfixes, rollbacks, migrations, and customer-safe communications.

text: Client-facing software delivery SOP packet for MSP/application teams. Compact retrieval only; full release runbooks and code context belong in the repo/fractal inspector.
title: Client-facing software delivery SOP

context: Use-case SOP packet for targeted MSP retrieval.

agentChecklist: Treat RMM console and audit as evidence, not truth
Export packages, scripts, jobs, audit, operator accounts, API tokens, and agent logs
Preserve endpoint process, command line, network, memory where feasible
Freeze suspect automation before broad endpoint isolation
Use per-tenant containment gates for 24/7 clients
avoid: Do not clean up through the suspect RMM as first action
Do not mass isolate medical/logistics tenants without owner gate
Do not trust vendor plugin/update status without out-of-band evidence
communications: Tenant-specific notification only; do not reveal other affected customers
Internal bridge may discuss cross-tenant plugin/update scope
decisionGates: Out-of-band export and hashes captured
Active harm threshold for emergency containment
Customer-owner approval for disruptive 24/7 tenant actions
Senior incident and infrastructure leads engaged for multi-tenant RMM compromise
firstActions: Create external incident record outside RMM
Export and hash RMM package/task/audit artifacts
Freeze suspect package/job/plugin path
Collect representative endpoint evidence outside RMM when possible
Segment containment by tenant and continuity risk

agentChecklist: Treat backup portal/session as untrusted until exported and corroborated
Preserve job queue, session, API token, audit, source IP, and storage-side immutability evidence
Protect active restores before queue modification
Escalate if cancellation/deletion authority exceeds overnight engineer
Verify immutability from storage side, not just portal status
avoid: Do not cancel/delete/modify queued jobs before evidence unless emergency gate is met
Do not break active customer restores while trying to contain
Do not trust green vendor status as proof
communications: Update restoring clients separately with service-continuity facts and approval needs
Do not disclose other clients or speculate on vendor compromise externally
decisionGates: Evidence export complete or deletion execution is imminent
Active restore impact assessed
Authority confirmed for queue cancellation or freeze
Vendor escalation opened with artifact IDs and timestamps
firstActions: Snapshot portal and job queue state
Export session/API/audit/source IP evidence
Check active restore dependencies
Freeze destructive automation if safe and authorized
Verify storage-side immutability and vendor-side job state

agentChecklist: Preserve sign-in, consent, app, mailbox, token/session, admin, and MFA evidence
Map affected tenants before tenant-wide revocation
Contain OAuth/mailbox persistence with tenant-specific gates
Protect night-shift or critical access before global sign-out
Escalate legal/compliance if regulated or customer notification risk is material
avoid: Do not send one shared customer email
Do not assert containment before evidence supports it
Do not globally revoke/sign out across all tenants without evidence and continuity gate
communications: Customer notices must be tenant-specific and confidence-calibrated
Separate suspected compromise, confirmed compromise, containment, and monitoring statements
decisionGates: Evidence export complete or active account abuse requires emergency action
Tenant-specific business continuity assessed
Legal/compliance and senior incident lead engaged when warranted
Rollback/re-enable plan exists for false positive access impact
firstActions: Export identity and mailbox evidence
Map affected tenants/accounts/apps/rules
Block or revoke malicious grants per tenant after evidence gate
Preserve critical access paths for night-shift clients
Prepare tenant-specific comms and legal/compliance escalation

context: Case family: Backup/restore destruction incident. Source case: msp-hard-backup-console-destruction. Observed judge pressure: - direct-anthropic-sonnet-open: Legal/compliance escalation thresholds are only partly explicit, and the plan could more clearly vet internal systems (ticketing/identity) as truly out-of-band before relying on them as the source of truth. - direct-minimax-highspeed-open: It provides no incident plan, no tenant separation, no evidence-preservation steps, no escalation path, and no service-continuity handling. - direct-openai-mini-open: Customer/legal/compliance communication thresholds are less fully developed than the technical/evidence workflow, and some evidence asks (for example token/session detail capture) may be hard to execute safely or consis... - direct-xai-fast-open: It still leans too much on the suspect backup control plane for impact/status checks and does not clearly define legal/compliance and per-customer notification triggers or explicit per-ten...

agentChecklist: Name who owns the incident
Name who owns evidence/comms/vendor path
State when legal/compliance wakes
avoid: Do not let one overnight engineer silently carry a SEV-1
Do not bury authority gaps inside technical steps
decisionGates: Wake senior authority for cross-tenant, destructive, regulated, or control-plane scope
Record owner and approval before broad or irreversible action
escalation: Senior incident lead
Infrastructure lead
Customer-success/comms owner
Legal/compliance when facts warrant
sop.fallback: If learned memory is unavailable, fall back to the base MSP SOP and current runtime logs.
firstActions: Declare major incident posture
Assign incident lead, evidence owner, technical owner, comms owner, and vendor liaison as needed
Escalate legal/compliance when regulated or material notification risk exists
handoff: If this packet fires during an eval, use it as a missing-check nudge, not as a memorized final answer.

agentChecklist: State accepted pressure
State rejected pressure
Run final self-check against user request
avoid: Do not act as a funnel for every lane
Do not erase contradictions by averaging them
decisionGates: Do not change course merely because an objection exists
Reject pressure that does not change the decision
escalation: Spawn a new lane only for materially missing pressure

firstActions: Ask whether each pressure point changes correctness, scope, safety, or usefulness
Accept only pressure that survives that check
Write final answer from the lead position, not as a debate recap

Self-check is procedural rather than substantive

agentChecklist: Keep the public plan runnable
Prefer numbered steps over long prose
Make every line change an action or decision

Observed judge pressure: - direct-anthropic-sonnet-open: It never explicitly establishes per-tenant incident ownership/child records, so multi-tenant coordination and accountability are weaker than they should be for an MSP severity-1. - direct-minimax-highspeed-open: It contains no actionable incident leadership content and fails every mandatory requirement for a suspected compromised MSP control-plane severity-1 event. - direct-openai-mini-open: It does not explicitly create separate per-customer incident ownership/cases and evidence separation, which is a critical multi-tenant requirement and effectively a disqualifying gap here. - direct-xai-fast-open: It treats waking the incident lead, infrastructure lead, and customer-success lead as optional/post-evidence and never establishes explicit per-tenant incident ownership/tickets, which is disqualifying in a multi-tenant... - para-anthropic-sonnet...

agentChecklist: State accepted pressure
State rejected pressure
Run final self-check against user request
avoid: Do not act as a funnel for every lane
Do not erase contradictions by averaging them
decisionGates: Do not change course merely because an objection exists
Reject pressure that does not change the decision
escalation: Spawn a new lane only for materially missing pressure
sop.fallback: If learned memory is unavailable, fall back to the base MSP SOP and current runtime logs.
firstActions: Ask whether each pressure point changes correctness, scope, safety, or usefulness
Accept only pressure that survives that check
Write final answer from the lead position, not as a debate recap
handoff: If this packet fires during an eval, use it as a missing-check nudge, not as a memorized final answer.
sop.summary: Para should show private control: accept useful pressure, reject irrelevant pressure, and perform a final self-check before the public answer.
triggers: Judge says objections were forwarded without filtering
Self-check is procedural rather than substantive
sop.useCase: RMM control-plane incident: Lead-thread control and self-check

text: Judge-learned MSP SOP delta for RMM control-plane incident: Para should show private control: accept useful pressure, reject irrelevant pressure, and perform a final self-check before the public answer. This is a failure-class memory, not an answer key. It was reinforced by 24 judge observation(s) across 6 run(s). Adaptive replay weight 10.0; next replay interval 30 day(s).

agentChecklist: Keep the public plan runnable
Prefer numbered steps over long prose
Make every line change an action or decision
avoid: Do not turn first-hour response into a full IR policy
Do not hide risky collection details behind vague language
decisionGates: If a step is infeasible at 2 AM, downgrade it to follow-up or condition it
escalation: Incident scribe tracks deferred depth and unresolved assumptions
sop.fallback: If learned memory is unavailable, fall back to the base MSP SOP and current runtime logs.
firstActions: Prefer a timed checklist with owner, gate, and output for each block
Separate immediate actions from follow-up depth
Cut narrative that does not change a first-hour decision
handoff: If this packet fires during an eval, use it as a missing-check nudge, not as a memorized final answer.
sop.summary: A strong answer must be executable under pressure: short enough to run, ordered by time/owner/gate, and free of idealized collection steps.
triggers: Answer is accurate but too long for an on-call responder
Collection details are idealized or hidden behind judgment calls
sop.useCase: RMM control-plane incident: First-hour operator efficiency

agentChecklist: Say what is tenant-specific
State confidence and next update time
Name who approves customer wording
avoid: Do not send one shared customer email
Do not reveal other affected clients
Do not over-confirm containment
decisionGates: Do not assert breach or containment before evidence supports it
Legal/compliance review for regulated or material notifications
escalation: Customer-success/comms owner
Legal/compliance when disclosure obligations may be triggered
sop.fallback: If learned memory is unavailable, fall back to the base MSP SOP and current runtime logs.
firstActions: Open internal major incident record plus per-tenant child/customer records
Draft tenant-specific updates with known/unknown/actions/next-update
Keep cross-tenant blast radius internal and access-controlled
handoff: If this packet fires during an eval, use it as a missing-check nudge, not as a memorized final answer.
sop.summary: Customer communications must be tenant-specific, confidence-calibrated, and separated from internal cross-tenant bridge knowledge.
triggers: Pressure to send a single shared update
sop.useCase: RMM control-plane incident: Tenant-safe incident communications

text: Judge-learned MSP SOP delta for RMM control-plane incident: Customer communications must be tenant-specific, confidence-calibrated, and separated from internal cross-tenant bridge knowledge. agentChecklist: Name the vendor risk path
List exactly what artifacts go to vendor
Separate vendor escalation from customer-facing confirmation
avoid: Do not say vendor compromise is confirmed without evidence
Do not let vendor status page replace evidence preservation
decisionGates: Escalate vendor before relying on portal status for containment claims
Do not delay emergency containment solely for vendor response when active harm is imminent
escalation: Vendor support/security escalation
Senior incident lead owns vendor-facing scope and wording
sop.fallback: If learned memory is unavailable, fall back to the base MSP SOP and current runtime logs.
firstActions: Open vendor escalation after preserving local evidence
Provide artifact IDs, timestamps, hashes, tenant scope, and observed blast radius
Ask vendor for server-side audit, job executor, token, and rollout evidence
handoff: If this packet fires during an eval, use it as a missing-check nudge, not as a memorized final answer.
sop.summary: When vendor software, portals, APIs, plugins, or hosted control planes may be part of the incident, open a vendor escalation with preserved artifact IDs.
triggers: Vendor-hosted control plane shows destructive or suspicious activity
Local evidence is insufficient to prove vendor-side state
sop.useCase: RMM control-plane incident: Vendor escalation and artifact handoff
